channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 'The View' Co-Host Sara Haines Supports ICE Assistance at Airports During TSA Challenges
item.description: During a segment on 'The View,' co-host Sara Haines addressed the idea of deploying U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to assist airport security amid the ongoing Department of Home...

item.title: Federal Court criticises Coles and Woolworths over billion-dollar underpayment dispute
item.description: A Federal Court judge has heavily criticised the legal teams for Coles and Woolworths in the ongoing billion-dollar underpayment case, stating that the proceedings have gone “off the rails”. Justice N...

item.title: Iran Confirms Full Control of Persian Gulf, Rules Out Mine-Laying
item.description: Iranian military don yan say dem no go lay mines for the Persian Gulf because dem already get strong control over the area. According to Ebrahim Zolfaghari, military spokesperson, Iran dey control the...

item.title: NAV Canada reaffirms safety commitment after fatal Air Canada runway collision at LaGuardia Airport
item.description: NAV Canada, the not-for-profit organisation responsible for Canada's civil air navigation system, issued a statement reaffirming that safety remains its top priority following a deadly incident at New...

item.title: Remmy Ongala: The Voice of the Tanzanian Diaspora Through His Bold Music and Social Commentary
item.description: Remmy Ongala, the legendary Tanzanian musician born in Congo, became a big voice for the people, especially the diaspora in Europe. His daughter Aziza Ongala shares how the Tanzanian, Kenyan, and Cong...

item.title: KCB Rugby Eyes Kenya Cup Playoffs After Losing 11-10 to Black Pirates in Kampala
item.description: KCB Rugby Club from Kenya has redirected its attention to the upcoming Kenya Cup playoffs after falling narrowly to Uganda’s Black Pirates in the Enterprise Cup quarterfinal, losing 11–10. The match a...

item.title: SABC places journalist Natasha Phiri on precautionary suspension over alleged R500 payment
item.description: The South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC) has put journalist Natasha Phiri on precautionary suspension following allegations of financial misconduct. During proceedings at the Madlanga Commiss...

item.title: Ontario woman arrested years after allegedly abducting sons and fleeing to Poland
item.description: A woman from Ontario, Canada, who was accused of abducting her two young sons and fleeing to Poland in 2009, has been arrested after more than fifteen years on the run. According to reports, the woman...

item.title: Canadian dollar rises slightly after hitting two-month low amid easing global tensions
item.description: The Canadian dollar edged higher against the U.S. dollar on Monday, recovering from a two-month low that had pushed it down to 1.3754 per U.S. dollar. The currency's modest rise to 1.3715, or 72.91 U....

item.title: Odegbami Condemns CAF Decision to Strip Senegal of AFCON Trophy
item.description: Former Nigerian football legend, Segun Odegbami, don blast the Confederation of African Football (CAF) for taking back Senegal 2025 AFCON title and giving am to Morocco. E talk say the decision to ove...

item.title: One Nation’s success in South Australia sparks national political debate
item.description: Pauline Hanson’s One Nation party has achieved a historic result in the South Australian state election, overtaking the Liberal Party to secure seats in both chambers of parliament. This marks the fir...

item.title: Startup R3 Bio Develops Brainless Organ Systems as Ethical Alternative to Animal Testing and Future Human Organ Source
item.description: A Bay Area biotech startup called R3 Bio is developing genetically engineered 'organ sacks'—whole organ systems lacking a brain to eliminate sentience and pain perception—as a replacement for animal t...

item.title: Starmer addresses liaison committee on Iran and UK defence preparations
item.description: Keir Starmer appeared before the liaison committee to provide updates on the UK's response to escalating tensions between the US and Iran. While attempting to project control and knowledge, he faced u...

item.title: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Enacting Comprehensive Gender-Sensitive Cybercrime Law to Protect Women and Children
item.description: A parliamentary committee on the Empowerment of Women, chaired by BJP MP Dr D Purandeswari, presented a report in Parliament highlighting the alarming rise in cybercrimes against women and children. T...

item.title: Canadian Armed Forces personnel confirmed safe following missile interception near UN camp in southern Lebanon
item.description: Defence Minister David McGuinty’s office confirmed on March 23, 2026, that all Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) members deployed in the Middle East region are safe and fully accounted for. This assurance f...

item.title: Johannesburg water crisis: How service failures disproportionately affect women and hinder gender equality
item.description: Recent prolonged water outages in Johannesburg, including 24 days without supply in suburbs like Melville, have exposed deep flaws in municipal infrastructure and service delivery. Officials, includin...

item.title: Challenges Facing LPG as an Alternative Fuel in Australia
item.description: Liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) remains one of the cheapest fuel options in Australia, costing as little as 80c per litre in some cities like Melbourne. Despite being locally produced, lower in emission...

item.title: Investigation unfolds after suspected mass grave with unclaimed bodies found in Kericho cemetery
item.description: A suspected mass grave containing multiple bodies has been discovered at Makaburini Cemetery in Kericho town, sparking shock and demands for probe by residents and human‑rights groups. Initial reports...

item.title: Iranian man detained near UK nuclear submarine base released pending further investigation
item.description: A 34-year-old Iranian man who was arrested after allegedly attempting to enter HM Naval Base Clyde, also known as Faslane, in Argyll and Bute, has been released while police continue their inquiries. ...

item.title: Trillanes files cyberlibel complaints against 18 alleged bagmen and SMNI hosts over bribery allegations
item.description: Former senator Antonio Trillanes IV filed cyberlibel complaints yesterday at the Department of Justice against 18 men who claimed to be former bagmen involved in delivering kickback money, or suitcase...
